page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-4xCPU-16GB is a general-purpose virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The configuration includes 4 shared vCPUs, 4 physical cores with 1 thread per core, and 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks indicate an entry-to-mid-tier profile. While single-core stress-ng performance is in the bottom 10% and multi-core performance is in the bottom 25%, the system achieves average results in Passmark CPU tests with a score of 7941.3 and Geekbench single-core tests with a score of 1321.0. Memory bandwidth tests show a stark contrast: small-block cached reads perform at an average rate of 410.01 GB/sec, whereas large-block uncached writes drop to a poor 14.27 GB/sec.

The shared CPU allocation introduces potential resource contention, which is reflected in the weaker multi-core and database benchmark results. For instance, Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput both fall into the bottom 25% tier. However, the server offers a cost-effective option for workloads that do not require sustained, intensive multi-core execution, balancing a standard 1:4 CPU-to-RAM ratio with a lower-cost shared hardware model.

This server is suited for general-purpose applications with moderate resource demands. Appropriate workloads include lightweight web serving, basic text and image processing, single-threaded compression tasks, and small-scale development or testing environments. It is less suitable for high-throughput database operations, heavy multi-threaded compilation, or medium-to-large language model inference.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-4xCPU-16GB is a general-purpose virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with 4 shared vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 16.0 GB of RAM, utilizing an AMD EPYC 7542 processor with an x86_64 architecture. The server does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data reveals average single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1321.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 7941.3, though multi-core and database operations show weaker results due to the shared CPU allocation. Memory bandwidth is average for small cached blocks but drops significantly for large uncached blocks. This configuration represents a cost-efficient option for workloads with moderate resource demands. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, basic image and text processing, and development environments rather than intensive database operations or heavy multi-threaded tasks.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-4xCPU-16GB is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and no local storage, offering cost-effective hosting for moderate general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-4xCPU-16GB is a KVM-based virtual server with 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 16 GB RAM, designed for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A cost-effective virtual server featuring shared AMD EPYC processors and balanced memory for moderate general-purpose and development workloads.
bullet_points: 4 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory
Average single-core performance suitable for basic processing tasks
No bundled local storage or GPU hardware
Best suited for lightweight web serving, development, and testing
